title: Ibuprofen Dosage Calculator
tagline: Estimate a typical ibuprofen single dose, liquid volume, or tablet count using weight, patient type, and dosing schedule.
intro: Enter weight, choose child or adult dosing, and select liquid or tablet form to estimate a typical per-dose amount and the maximum daily limit.

error.invalidWeight: Enter a valid weight greater than zero.
error.invalidConcentration: Enter a valid liquid concentration greater than zero.
error.invalidTablet: Enter a valid tablet strength greater than zero.
error.invalidSchedule: Enter a valid schedule for doses per day and interval.

about.heading: About this ibuprofen dose tool
about.body:
Ibuprofen is a nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug, often shortened to NSAID, that is commonly used to reduce pain, inflammation, and fever. In practical use, people often need help translating a weight-based recommendation into a volume of liquid medicine or a number of tablets. That is the main purpose of this calculator. It uses a typical pediatric single-dose estimate of 10 mg per kilogram and compares that estimate with a daily ceiling of 40 mg per kilogram divided across the planned number of doses. For adults, it uses a common 400 mg single dose and a maximum daily amount of 3200 mg. The result is meant to be a quick reference, not a substitute for a clinician who knows the patient’s history, kidney function, stomach risk, hydration status, and concurrent medicines.

In children, the most important variables are weight, the medication concentration, and how often the medicine will be given. Pediatric products are frequently expressed as milligrams per milliliter, and a small error in concentration can create a large difference in the final measured volume. That is why the calculator separates the dose in milligrams from the dose in milliliters. The milligram result tells you the underlying target dose, while the milliliter result translates that number into a practical amount to measure. If you switch between products, always re-check the concentration on the package instead of assuming two bottles contain the same strength.

For adults, ibuprofen dosing is not usually calculated directly from weight in routine over-the-counter situations, but weight is still included here because caregivers often think about both children and adults within the same workflow. The adult setting returns a typical 400 mg single dose and a 3200 mg daily maximum. In real care settings, many adults use lower total daily amounts, especially when symptoms are mild or short-lived. The safest effective dose is usually preferred. A person with stomach ulcers, kidney disease, heart failure, dehydration, pregnancy concerns, or blood-thinner use may need a different plan entirely.

This calculator is most useful as a structured dose-conversion aid. It helps you move from a weight-based estimate to either milliliters or tablets while still showing the daily maximum as a safety check. It does not judge whether ibuprofen is the right medicine, whether it should be alternated with another medication, or whether the patient has warning signs that need urgent evaluation. Severe pain, repeated vomiting, dehydration, black stools, breathing trouble, or suspected overdose all require professional advice right away.

examples.heading: Ibuprofen dosing examples
examples.intro: These example scenarios show how weight, formulation, and schedule affect the estimated result.

items.output: 180 mg per dose → 9 mL; 720 mg/day max
items.note: Typical pediatric example where the 10 mg/kg single dose stays below the daily cap.

items.output: 250 mg per dose → 12.5 mL; 1000 mg/day max
items.note: Giving four doses still keeps the total within the 40 mg/kg/day ceiling.

items.output: 400 mg per dose → 2 tablets; 3200 mg/day max
items.note: A common adult tablet example using a typical 400 mg single dose.

howto.heading: How to use it
steps: Enter the patient’s current weight in kilograms and add age if you want it recorded in the calculation form.
Choose whether you are estimating a child dose or a standard adult dose.
Select liquid or tablet form, then enter either the liquid concentration or tablet strength shown on the package.
Set the planned number of doses per day and the interval in hours, then click Calculate to review the per-dose amount and daily maximum.

faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
faq.q1: Why does the child result depend on doses per day?
faq.q1_a: The pediatric estimate uses 10 mg/kg for a single dose, but it is also clamped so the total daily amount does not exceed 40 mg/kg/day when split across the chosen number of doses. This means that for very frequent dosing schedules the per-dose amount may be reduced below the standard 10 mg/kg target.
faq.q2: Why do I need the concentration for liquid ibuprofen?
faq.q2_a: Different liquid products can have different strengths. The concentration converts the milligram target into a measurable number of milliliters.
faq.q3: Does this calculator tell me if ibuprofen is safe for this patient?
faq.q3_a: No. It only estimates dose size. Safety depends on age, kidney function, stomach risk, hydration, pregnancy status, and many other clinical factors.
faq.q4: Why is the adult single dose fixed at 400 mg?
faq.q4_a: The adult setting reflects a common single dose used for pain or fever. Real-world recommendations may be lower or more individualized depending on the situation.
faq.q5: Should I use this tool for suspected overdose?
faq.q5_a: No. If too much ibuprofen may have been taken, contact a poison center or urgent medical service immediately instead of relying on this estimate.
